content: Yeah, so dinner last night was pretty awkward. When they invited me to talk about the project, I kind of froze up. I just nodded along because I didn’t want them to think less of me, and then when I said something I wasn’t sure about, they just laughed and went off on a tangent. Honestly, it felt like everyone was judging me, and I guess I just wanted to fit in somehow. It’s hard to stop laughing at things I don’t even find funny. I wish I could say no, but I’m scared they’ll think less of me. It’s like, if I show any weakness, they’re going to see how ridiculous I am. I feel like maybe if I kept trying to impress everyone, they’d just leave me. It’s exhausting sometimes trying to seem interested or interesting. I guess I’m afraid of disappointing anyone, which makes me feel like I’m already doomed anyway. So, I keep pushing back and agreeing to whatever seems easiest, even though I’m uncomfortable with what I’m saying. It’s frustrating because I want to be seen as someone worth being around. But honestly, I’m scared of getting left behind or losing them. I appreciate it when people let me know I’m appreciated, but I’m also okay with sticking to myself even if it means being quiet sometimes. It’s like this person wants me to prove myself, and I’m just stuck in the middle. I’ve tried harder lately, but it doesn’t seem to help much. It’s exhausting to navigate through these situations where I’m constantly feeling like I’m not cut out for them. So, yeah, I mostly just numb it all out. I don’t really know why, but it feels like there’s this part inside me that just refuses to let go of the feeling of being excluded.

Yeah, this past week really hit me hard. I noticed they’ve been less talking, staying behind more than usual, maybe it’s just showing how much they care about me but aren’t there anymore. I keep thinking, “Why bother trying to connect if I’m not going to make them happy?” And honestly, it makes me feel like I’m never enough, like I’m always letting people down. It’s exhausting, especially when things just seem to go wrong again. I guess I just feel like I’m making a fool of myself, which makes me even more anxious. Sometimes I wonder if they’re just waiting for someone else to leave. I need to stop feeling this way though, but I don’t know how to stop. It feels like no matter what I do, it just doesn’t work out the way I hoped. I’m afraid if I start feeling more connected or if I lose them, then it’ll all end in disappointment. It’s frustrating because I want to stay close and support them, but I’m scared of what that means. So, I mostly just shut down or pull away from them, hoping it’ll be okay. But then I wake up feeling worse, knowing it won’t be as good as I thought. It’s like they’re already saying goodbye anyway, and I’m just stuck in that place.

Yeah, so... last week was kind of rough when they gave me that big project. Honestly, I just kept thinking, “I’m sure they’re right, maybe I’ll screw everything up.” Then, after trying so hard to plan things out, I ended up putting off doing it because I felt like it wouldn’t make any difference or that people would just see how inadequate I really am. I’ve been staying home from work lately because I don’t want to face those chances of showing my lack of preparation or not knowing how to handle things properly. It’s frustrating because I know there’s something wrong but just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I’m failing. I guess I feel pretty stuck in my head even though I’m struggling to keep up. It’s like no matter how much effort I put in, it’s never enough. I wish I could just get some clarity about why I’m having trouble handling this project. It makes me feel really anxious and sad at the same time.

It was really hard meeting people at the party. When I saw everyone nodding off and some people were whispering, I just felt like maybe they’re thinking I’m weird or that I’m boring them. I kept trying to join in, but then I’d feel this overwhelming urge to smile and just pretend to understand because if I didn’t, they might think I’m just being annoying or silly. After a while, I just stayed away, mostly because I wanted to avoid getting too caught up in the conversation and just kind of disappear. It’s exhausting, but I guess I’m scared of disappointing anyone or sounding too obvious. I wish I could talk more, but sometimes my mind just wanders. I’ll admit to staying out early, but honestly, I just feel like I’m going to mess everything up. It’s like no matter how many chances I get, I always fall short of expectations. I’ve been thinking a lot lately about why I never seem to connect with people easily—I wonder if there’s something wrong with me. It’s frustrating because I want to be part of the group, but I just feel like I’m missing out. So, I prefer to be invisible or not participate at all. It’s like I’m stuck between wanting to fit in and fearing rejection. I guess I need someone to trust me enough to let me in and feel like I belong. It’s tough for me to admit that I’m not good enough, but then I just feel ashamed or like I’m not enough. I hope they’ll notice how strange I am or think I’m not interesting enough. It’s exhausting but also incredibly satisfying to feel safe around people. I appreciate the question you asked, but I’m afraid I won’t answer unless I’m forced to. Let me know if I’ve hit the right spot!

Yeah, this week was really tough. When we started talking about how I want something different, I just felt like my voice wasn’t enough, and then it all went down the drain. I kept thinking, “What if I’m just not cut out for this?” Or maybe I’m just too stubborn to get what I want. It’s exhausting, especially since it’s part of something bigger that’s holding us together. I guess sometimes I feel like I’m trapped inside this relationship, and I don’t know how to break free or change things. It’s like, no matter how much I try, it just doesn’t work out the way I planned. I’ve tried to figure out ways to communicate better, but nothing seems to help. It feels like every time I push back, it just makes me feel worse or harder to stand. So, I’ve decided to just go along with whatever’s happening rather than fight it or try anything else. It’s hard to shake off this feeling of being trapped and powerless, but I don’t want to lose this connection either. It’s like I’m afraid of letting anyone see how deeply I’m stuck. I’ll admit, though, sometimes I wish I could step outside of this and give it a shot. But honestly, that scares me instead. It’s like I’m stuck in this place where I can’t move forward. So, I’m glad I’m doing this alone right now—though I worry that I’m just proving that I’m incapable. It’s frustrating, but I don’t want to let someone else take away the love I have here. Please, don’t let me down. I need someone to tell me what’s really going on. Thank you for being here. I appreciate you understanding how hard this is for me. I really want to talk about it, but I’m scared I’ll just make things worse. I’m sorry

Yeah, this past weekend just felt really overwhelming. When they started talking about career plans and lifestyle changes that didn’t fit, I just froze up because I kept thinking, “There’s no way out.” It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I can’t move forward, even though it’s clearly something I want to do differently. I’ve been avoiding most of those conversations because honestly, I don’t want to start another one that’ll only make things worse. Sometimes I wonder if people really care what I choose, or if they’re just waiting for me to give up. It’s hard to connect with them when I feel so isolated and lost inside. I guess I just don’t know how to break free or change anything on my own. It’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending cycle of trying to find my place, but instead, I end up feeling more alone and less capable. It’s frustrating and drains me deeply. I’d rather stay home than face all that pressure and frustration. Just imagine being able to say, “I need some space,” or maybe just sit down and do something else I enjoy. That would be much easier than feeling like I’m stuck here. So, it’s like I’m trapped in this pattern where I’m constantly questioning why I’m stuck in this place. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how to stop. I guess I just need someone to tell me what’s really bothering me and show me ways to get out of this mess. Or at least let me know I’m not going to make it any better. It’s scary to ask for help, but I’m afraid I’ll just prove to everyone I’m incapable anyway. It feels safer to just shut down and pretend nothing’s wrong. But then I end up feeling even worse, reinforcing my idea that I’m doomed. It’s exhausting, but I can’t seem to shake

I’ve been feeling really frustrated lately because this whole family thing seems to be pushing me around more than I want. I feel like no matter what I try, it just doesn’t cut it, and then I’m left feeling useless and alone. I just shut down when things get tough, hoping it’ll stop, but it keeps replaying everything the same. Honestly, it’s exhausting trying to push back or figure out my own path. Sometimes I wonder if maybe I’m better off staying home rather than face all that pressure alone. It’s like everyone’s always looking for reasons why I can’t do something right. I guess I just don’t know how to say no anymore. It’s exhausting, and I wish I could break free somehow. But I’m scared of losing myself or falling further into the trap of being trapped. It’s frustrating because I want to grow and express my own needs, but they’re met with silence or criticism instead. I feel like I’m failing to measure up to what they expect from me. It’s hard to shake off these recurring thoughts that remind me of how unworthy I feel. I’d prefer to just disappear somewhere safe, but I don’t want to leave the safety of my own life just to deal with this stress. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m constantly worrying about whether I’m succeeding or not, even though I’m already questioning everything. I need someone to understand how deeply this is affecting me, but I’m afraid to ask or admit how I’m truly struggling. I’m sorry for bringing this up, but I just don’t feel ready to talk about it. It feels like I’m putting myself through another round of disappointment. Please help me connect with someone who understands how overwhelming this feels. I appreciate you thinking about me this way, and I really need this conversation to ease some of this pain. Thank you for letting me

Yeah, this time was pretty rough. When they started talking about things differently than usual, I just felt like I had to kind of follow along because I didn’t want them to think less of me or lose them. I kept agreeing with everything, even when it wasn’t true, because if I said something different, they’d probably say I’m wrong or don’t care anymore. It’s like I’m trapped inside myself trying to please them instead of actually connecting or saying what I truly believe. Honestly, sometimes I wonder why I can’t stop from being a little bit more independent. It’s exhausting knowing I’m doing nothing to change anything. I guess I feel like if I ever show my own ideas, they’ll turn out better than what they’re giving me. So, I just stick to what they tell me. It’s frustrating because I know deep down I’m not cut out for this type of relationship stuff. But then again, maybe I’m too old-fashioned for it anyway. At least I’ve figured out some ways to keep pushing through.

Yeah, it was really hard when everyone started talking again. I guess I just didn’t feel like I belonged there. It felt like no one really cared what I had to say—like maybe I wasn’t even interesting to them. I ended up just staying where nobody was looking because I didn’t want to start any trouble or seem like I needed attention. Honestly, I’ve been thinking a lot lately that maybe people aren’t really interested in me after all. It’s exhausting, but it’s also frustrating knowing that I’m constantly feeling left out. I guess I just don’t know how to connect with people unless I’m actively trying. It’s like I’m stuck in this place where I can’t move forward. So, I prefer to just disappear. It’s hard to face the truth sometimes, especially when I feel like I’m just not good enough. I appreciate it when people acknowledge my struggles though—it helps me realize I need help. But honestly, I’m scared of falling apart if I admit too much. So instead, I just stick to myself and hope it works out okay. It’s frustrating, but I don’t want to break anything else. I’ll be back later, maybe after dinner, but right now, I just feel worse than before. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s never going to make a difference.

Yeah, this week was really tough. When I looked at my budget, I just felt like... nothing’s there to justify how I’m spending money. I kept thinking, “Why bother trying when I can’t even get by?” It’s like no matter how hard I work, something still just fails to make sense. I haven’t done much searching for jobs because I don’t want to admit I’m struggling anymore. I’ve been avoiding talking about my job or financial stuff too much, just hoping it’ll stop bothering me. Honestly, feeling overwhelmed and ashamed is overwhelming sometimes. Sometimes I wonder if maybe I’m just not cut out for life. It’s like I’m always falling short of expectations, and I’m afraid to prove I’m capable enough. I guess I feel stuck in this cycle where I can’t move forward, and I’m scared I’ll never make things better. It’s frustrating because I want to start earning money again, but I just can’t seem to find the right path. Maybe if I just gave up completely, I’d be closer to being okay. But then again, maybe that just shows how deeply I’m stuck. I wish there were some magic solution or reason why I can’t afford everything. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I don’t want to face anything bigger than this. So, I just feel trapped in this place where I need help, but I don’t think I’m ready for it. It’s like I’m afraid to step outside or say what’s really happening. I’m sorry if this feels like I’m not being truthful. Please, let me in. I just need someone to understand how deeply I’m stuck here. Thank you for asking. I appreciate you wanting to talk to me though. It’s reassuring knowing you’re here for me. I’ll be honest next time. I promise I won’t let you down.

I’ve been thinking about applying for that promotion lately because it seems like everyone already knows about it. But honestly, I’m scared that if I make a mistake, they’ll realize I’m not cut out for this position. I keep putting off doing it because I worry that showing up awkwardly or failing would prove I’m not valuable or worthy of being promoted. It’s hard to focus on something so important when I’m worried about how much I’d mess up. I guess sometimes just avoiding talking about it helps me feel safe and less exposed. It’s frustrating because even though I know it’s wrong, I don’t want to show anyone else that I’m struggling. I just feel like I’m letting them down too. It’s like I’m always falling short, and I don’t want to break any bonds or disappoint anyone. So, I end up delaying the application just to avoid all those worries. It’s exhausting knowing that I’m going against my own sense of worthiness.

Yeah, so today when they gave me that big project, it felt like right away everything seemed like it was going to fall apart. I kept thinking, “What’s the point of even trying? Everyone will notice how dumb I am.” Then, when I tried to plan it, I just shut down and didn’t want to start. I guess I’m scared that if I try, I’ll just mess something up or show everyone what a failure I am. It’s like no matter how hard I try, I just can’t get through to anyone or figure out how to move forward. I guess I’m just blaming myself for never having better skills or being prepared enough. It makes me feel even more defeated and useless after all this pressure. Honestly, it’s hard to imagine that maybe I’m actually capable of handling this kind of weighty responsibility.

Yeah, so this time at the office thing… it felt like everyone was really looking at me. I guess I just kept trying to fit in, even if it made no sense to anyone around me. I didn’t say much because I wanted to keep things simple, but then I felt like maybe people were just dismissing me somehow. After a while, I just went away early because I started feeling overwhelmed and kind of lost myself. It’s hard to break out of that loop where I feel like there’s something wrong with me. I guess I’m afraid if I show my true self, people will see me as just not worth being around anymore. It’s frustrating because I want to connect but also feel safe and important. So, I ended up leaving earlier than planned. Coming back later seemed pointless—like I wouldn’t prove anything. It’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending cycle of feeling invisible or useless. I wish I could talk more freely, but it’s harder than I thought.

Yeah, so... I was at our dinner last night, and my partner started talking about all this stuff, like they’re worried I’m taking too much care of work. It made me feel really butterflies in my stomach, kind of like, “What if I don’t help?” And then I just said something fast and apologized, promising to try harder tomorrow to make it right. But then after that, I felt even worse, and honestly, I just wanted to shut down and pretend nothing happened. It’s like, maybe I’m the one who’s failing them? Or maybe I’m just being a fool. I guess I’ve been thinking a lot about what I did earlier, and it makes me feel worse because I’m afraid I’m making things worse. I wish I could talk to someone else about it instead of just keeping secrets. It’s hard to stop worrying about whether I’ll let them know I’m upset when they start complaining. So, yeah, I ended up staying late that night and apologizing anyway, but I feel pretty drained afterwards. It’s like, sometimes I just want to disappear completely from them.

Yeah, so... I was thinking about asking to lead that new project last week, and honestly, just feeling kind of stuck there right now. I keep thinking, “What if someone notices I’m not prepared enough?” Or maybe even worse, like, “Wow, what did I really expect? I’ll never make it through that.” It’s exhausting, but I guess part of me just wants to avoid all that. And then I spend so much time rehearsing everything again, barely sleeping because it feels overwhelming. I don’t know, it’s like I’m scared of falling down or embarrassing myself too much. I mean, come on, I’ve got this big chance here, why wouldn’t I want to take it? But then I worry that if I mess things up, people will think less of me or say I’m not cut out for this job. It’s hard to shake off this nagging feeling of being inadequate. I appreciate it when people acknowledge that sometimes our fears aren’t always obvious, though. It helps me see clearly what I’m afraid of before I act. So yeah, I guess I feel pretty anxious about leading that project, and it’s hard to not let go. But I’m also figuring that maybe it’s better to stay focused and not let myself fall apart. It’s a bit of a struggle, but I guess I just need to keep pushing forward until I can prove myself. I’ll be back next week, maybe. Maybe then I won’t be so overwhelmed. I’ll tell you, though, I wish I could get that project done faster. It’s easier said than done, but I’m determined to get it done.

Yeah, um, well, last night… when they picked the restaurant, I felt this tight knot in my stomach. Honestly, I was just so happy they stuck with what I said. But then, after agreeing, I kept thinking, “What if they don’t like me anyway?” It’s hard not to feel kind of sad or even a little ashamed when I’m forced to conform to them. I guess I just wanted to protect myself from the pain of maybe not getting what I wanted. And honestly, sometimes it makes me feel better to just pretend everything’s okay, right? I’m afraid if I showed any sign of wanting different places, they’ll start wondering why I’m different. It’s exhausting though—like trying to push through one thing without actually getting anywhere. I hope I can get out of this feeling stronger next time. Back to you. What did you think about that situation? I’d love to hear how you saw it and how it made you feel.

Yeah, this family thing last week just made everything really shake up for me. When they started talking about my career and life choices, I felt like I couldn’t keep up or say anything important. I kept thinking, “Why bother trying when everyone seems so dismissive?” It’s like no matter what I do, it’s never good enough for them. After I left the party, I just wanted to get out of there fast because it felt too much for me. I guess sometimes I feel like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop where things just don’t work out for me. It’s hard to stop feeling like maybe if I showed my opinions or tried harder, they’ll just reject me again. I wish I could figure out how to make them happier instead of just making them angry. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I’m scared of losing myself or being seen as less valuable. I appreciate it when people acknowledge my flaws, but I also want to feel like I’m capable and interesting enough to connect with them. It’s like I’m trapped inside a cycle where I always end up feeling like I don’t belong. So, I guess I need some help figuring out how to break free from this tightness. It’s frustrating because I know I shouldn’t be afraid to show my own strengths, but I just can’t stop doubting myself. I’m sorry if I’m not being honest or true about these feelings. I’m just tired of pretending to be something I’m not. Please help me understand what’s happening inside me better. It’s like I’m missing something crucial. I’d love for someone to see how truly broken I am and how I’ve let them down. Thank you for asking. I appreciate you understanding me. I really need some guidance here. I’m really looking forward to our next meeting where we can talk more openly and share how I’m doing. I

Yeah, this whole idea of having someone else visit family again was really scary. I keep thinking maybe they won’t like me when they’re there, and then everything gets messy because I’m too much of a bother. I’ve tried to change things around, but even then, it seems like it’ll never stop feeling like I’m going to lose them. It’s exhausting being constantly pushing my limits so I can get somewhere else. Honestly, I feel like if I don’t make them stay, maybe they’ll just decide to stay away because I’m annoying or something. So, I’m just hoping no one notices how hard I’m trying. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I need to be doing something right to avoid hurting them. But honestly, I just don’t know what to say. I guess I’m afraid of losing all the love I had for them. It’s frustrating because it’s like I’m supposed to be the one who’s lost, but instead, I’m feeling like I’m falling apart.

Yeah, this whole work stress really hit me lately. When I get a new project, I always just double-check everything again and again, hoping maybe someone’s going to mess up somehow. It’s like I’m afraid I’ll be seen as incompetent or maybe even worse than people around me. Honestly, I find myself just sitting on the floor most days after work because I don’t know what to do. Sometimes I worry that if I let myself fall behind, everyone will see how dumb I really am. It’s exhausting and makes me feel like I’m never good enough, though I guess I’ve tried too hard already. I wish I could just figure out how to stop worrying so much and just enjoy being here instead of constantly struggling. It’s frustrating because I want to keep pushing forward, but then I just end up falling apart. I’m sorry if I sound a bit grumpy or like I’m just blaming myself for all this. But honestly, sometimes it feels harder to connect with coworkers when I’m worried about what they might think. It’s like I’m trying to protect myself from getting hurt, but it’s just proving that I’m not strong enough as an individual. I need some guidance or maybe just a moment where I can just forget about it and relax. Can you imagine how much easier it would be if I just stopped trying? It’s like I’m stuck somewhere between being capable and failing. Thank you for asking. It’s really helping me sort through this. I’ll keep that experience in mind next time I face similar pressure.

Yeah, so this dinner was really hard for me. When everyone started talking about their careers, I just felt like maybe I wasn’t even keeping up, or worse, I was somehow falling behind. It’s like every time I say something important, people start saying less and looking at me differently, which makes me feel like I’m always the one being forgotten. I ended up just telling them I was too tired to go through it all, but honestly, it just made me want to run away. I don’t know, it’s like no matter how much I try, I just can’t make it out ahead of everyone else. It’s exhausting trying to pretend otherwise when inside I feel like I’m failing again. I guess I just feel like I’m putting myself on the back foot whenever things get tough. It’s hard to connect with people who seem better off than me. I wish there were some way to stop these recurring thoughts or feelings altogether. It’s like I need someone to see how messed up I am, but I don’t think I can trust anyone to understand or care about me unless I’m actually making progress. It’s frustrating because I want to prove that I’m capable, but then it feels like I’m just proving that I’m not good enough. So, I’ve decided to just leave earlier, maybe find a friend or a book to read instead. But I’m scared of coming back and having to deal with everything again. It’s a big struggle for me to connect with family members, and I just don’t think I can fix anything without losing some part of me. I hope my avoidance isn’t hurting me any deeper though. It feels like I’m stuck between wanting to belong and feeling like I’m alone. Please let me know if I sound too uprooted or off-putting. I really appreciate you understanding me this way. I’m sorry if this

Yeah, um, I guess I’ve been thinking about that party again lately. It feels like everyone’s already noticing how awkward I seem, and maybe they’re wondering why I’m there at all. I keep thinking, “What if they just invite me because they don’t want me here?” It’s exhausting trying to shake off the whole thing and feel like I’m just another reminder of what I’m missing. Honestly, I end up spending the night alone, just ruminating over old arguments and feeling kind of relieved that I didn’t really go. It’s hard to stop replaying those moments in my head. I don’t know, I just feel so drained and stuck in a place where nothing seems to work out for me. It’s like I’m afraid of losing something important just by being here. So, I ended up just saying no and heading home instead. It’s frustrating because I want to connect, but I just can’t seem to pull myself away from those lingering worries.

Yeah, so last night at the dinner, my mom was just saying how their sibling did something amazing, but then they didn’t even mention it, which made me feel like maybe I’m not special or worth being here. I just kind of shut down and didn’t say much because I didn’t want to start another argument or show off. After that, I kept thinking, “Why can’t it just be me? Why don’t they all seem to like each other?” It’s hard to shake this feeling of being truly useless and unworthy of love. I guess I just feel like I’m invisible or insignificant when things go wrong. It makes me want to leave sooner rather than try again. But honestly, sometimes staying put helps me figure out why I’m stuck in this place where I don’t belong.

Yeah, so… I guess I was thinking along the lines of joining that community thing because I felt like maybe if I showed up and said something different, maybe I’d just stir up some argument or make everyone confused. But then, honestly, when they started talking, I just stayed quiet, didn’t really say much, and kept agreeing too easily, which made me feel kind of invisible even though I was there. It’s like I’m afraid of losing whatever connection I had with them if I try to push myself out there. I guess feeling stuck and anxious all these emotions combined makes it harder to open up. Sometimes I wonder if being here isn’t actually helping me at all. I wish I could tell them how uncomfortable I am, but it’s hard not to stay closed off. So, I mostly just wait for opportunities to come around instead. It’s like, sometimes I forget that I’m already stuck and that no matter what I do, I can’t change anything about who I am.

Yeah, this week was kind of rough. When we were talking at work, I noticed some things just happened without me saying much. I felt like maybe people weren’t really paying attention because they didn’t seem to care about what I had to say. It made me feel invisible even though I’m usually quite involved in most conversations. After that, I just shut down, didn’t contribute much, and then left early. It’s hard to believe I’m still stuck here after all these years. Sometimes I wonder if there’s something wrong with me, like I’m just not cut out for these kinds of relationships. I guess I don’t know how to deal with being so easily forgotten or ignored. It’s like I’ve got a whole bunch of stuff I want to say, but I just can’t bring myself to share it. It’s exhausting and makes me feel like I’m not worth the effort. I’m sorry if I’m making this sound like an argument, but I just feel like I’m failing to connect with others. I wish I could figure out how to make myself more noticeable again. It’s frustrating because I want to be part of the team and help improve things. But I don’t know how to stop letting myself fall apart. I’ll admit, sometimes I wonder if I’m not cut out for life in this environment. I appreciate it when people notice me, but it’s harder to keep up when I’m constantly feeling invisible. It’s like I’m not even myself anymore. I guess I need someone to tell me I’m not good enough before I lose my place. I’m sorry if this seems like I’m being too obvious, but I’m scared of making a fool of myself or being rejected. It’s okay to feel this way, but I just feel like I need to prove myself first. I’m trying to remind myself that everyone else is probably just as busy

Yeah, this project came up again just yesterday, and I started worrying really hard. When I looked at the report, I saw all these spots that seemed like they were clearly not quite right, and I just kept double-checking everything. I kept thinking, “What if they don’t notice what I’ve done? Or maybe they’re already judging me anyway.” It’s exhausting trying to keep up, even though I know it’s unnecessary. I feel like I’m just making a mess of things, and no one seems to care unless I show exactly how I’m supposed to fix them. I guess I’m scared that if I say anything, people will think less of me or realize I’m not cut out for this kind of work. It’s frustrating because honestly, I want to help, but I just feel stuck in this cycle of fearing failure. It’s like, no matter how much I try, it never feels enough to prove myself enough. I’ll go through all those steps too many times, hoping to get rid of any doubts, but then they just come back. It’s exhausting being so sure I’m capable of doing this. I’m sorry if this feels like an anxiety attack—I just don’t know how else to explain it. Please, let me know if there’s anything I could do differently. I need some answers right now.
